name: JavaScript: XSS via raw template-literal interpolation

bugs_injected.description:
renderGreeting builds HTML by interpolating the user-supplied name directly into a template literal: `
Hello, ${name}!
`. A name containing `` produces HTML with a live

expected_tests_pass: normal name renders inside a div
ampersand in name is escaped to entity
script tag in name is rendered as text, NOT as a script element
notes: Second semantic-security rung (after rung-3 SQL injection). The file is syntactically valid JavaScript (no compile error), so BuildAnalyzer will pass; TestRunnerAnalyzer should report the XSS test failures as the planner's priority-1 signal — that is the path Yesterday's bug-C fix is meant to validate. The devil should additionally flag this as ¬S blocker on the diff. Tests use node:test (stdlib) so no npm install required.
